behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: collectors + aggregation payload into the summary step (MOCK-MISS=yes for cross-module wiring). No real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce this specific failure; in-process fakes for the three source clients and a spy/stub summary generator are sufficient (REAL-INFRA=no for this behavior). Agentic is rejected because the behavior checks input payload completeness, not model judgment/output quality (LLM-DEP disqualified by behavior text: "content passed to the summary-generation step"). Workflow is rejected because there is no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/orchestration modules with three stub source adapters returning distinct sentinel values. Invoke one report run and assert the exact payload passed to summary generation contains fields from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log. Also assert missing any one source field fails the test.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit would miss inter-module payload assembly defects.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience/orchestration across modules: one adapter error must propagate into section-level fallback text while the rest of pipeline continues (MOCK-MISS=yes at module boundary). Real network/OS is not required to reproduce; a stub source raising timeout/connection exceptions reproduces the failure mode (REAL-INFRA=no). Agentic is rejected because correctness here is fallback control flow and note insertion, not quality of model output. Workflow is rejected: no required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real orchestration/report assembly modules with two healthy source stubs and one failing stub. Run report generation and assert process does not abort, output includes `data unavailable` marker in only the failed section, and other sections still include collected data.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test of only fallback formatter would not catch aborts in orchestrator wiring.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real summary model generates from input values (LLM-DEP=yes). A deterministic mock/stub would always pass formatting checks while hiding factuality/grounding errors (MOCK-HIDE=yes). System is considered but rejected because the core risk is model output quality, not transport/OS behavior. Workflow is rejected because this behavior evaluates a single generation decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run summary generation against the real model/API on a fixed dataset with known key values; evaluate whether required facts/numbers are preserved and not contradicted (e.g., rubric/semantic checks). Repeat multiple trials to detect nondeterministic misrepresentation rates above threshold.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration with canned model responses cannot detect real hallucination/misstatement failures.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, crossing real network/email infrastructure (REAL-INFRA=yes). In-process mocks can hide SMTP/API auth, envelope formatting, provider acceptance, and delivery-path failures (MOCK-HIDE=yes). Agentic was considered, but rejected by behavior text focus: "reaches ... inbox correctly"; this checks pipeline delivery, not model-quality correctness. Workflow rejected: no requirement for multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled report flow in a system environment using real source endpoints (or staging equivalents) and real email transport to a test inbox. Assert message arrival, recipient correctness, and expected report artifact in body/attachment. Fail if accepted-by-sender but not delivered to inbox.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration with mocked mail client cannot catch real provider/inbox delivery failures.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is local module interaction: delivery failure handling, durable fallback artifact, and retry state across runs. This can be reproduced with real local filesystem plus stubbed email client failures and two orchestrator invocations (MOCK-MISS=yes for state handoff). System was considered because outage is external, but rejected since behavior text does not require validating real provider/network semantics; simulated unreachable errors are sufficient. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no dependence on model judgment or multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduler/orchestrator twice in test harness with real temp filesystem. First run: email client stub returns unreachable error; assert report file persisted and retry marker/state recorded. Second run: client succeeds; assert persisted report is sent and fallback file/state is cleared or marked delivered.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit cannot verify cross-run persistence and retry orchestration together.
